name: Network IoT Incident Response
description: Comprehensive playbook for responding to network and IoT security incidents including unauthorized access, compromised devices, and network-based attacks.

phases.name: Identification
phases.description: The purpose of the Identification stage is to accurately determine the Incident type, severity and perform immediate containment and escalation.

tasks.title: Initiate CIRT Team Meeting and Execute Escalation Procedures
tasks.content: As more information about the incident is known, the core CIRT team will meet and define which organizational SMEs need to be included to respond to the incident.
outcomes: First Core CIRT meeting is held with invited SMEs
OOB communications channels are set up and periodic checkins scheduled
Privileged Communications is established with legal counsel

subtasks.title: Initiate first CIRT meeting
subtasks.content: Initiated first meeting of the organizational core CIRT.

subtasks.title: Notify CIRT team members
subtasks.content: Notified CIRT team members using the contacts and role assignments on the My Team page and made sure ORNA roles are assigned so they are aware of the tasks allocated for them.

subtasks.title: Verify Incident Severity
subtasks.content: Verified the Incident Severity determined during the Incident record creation process as promptly and accurately as possible. The severity may change as more details are uncovered and it will need to be updated when the situation changes.

subtasks.title: Invite additional SMEs
subtasks.content: Invited additional SMEs to be included in CIRT update meetings.

subtasks.title: Set up secured meeting room
subtasks.content: Set up a secured meeting room (either physical and/or virtual).

subtasks.title: Schedule follow-up meetings
subtasks.content: Scheduled follow-up meetings according to a defined cadence.

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel to retain client-attorney privilege over the information pertaining to the incident and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Create initial incident assessment
subtasks.content: Created an initial incident assessment to share with organizational leaders. This assessment notification will contain a summary of what has occurred; date & time of incident; scope of compromise; data involved; actions taken so far and next steps recipients can expect to see.

subtasks.title: Establish log of meetings
subtasks.content: Established a log of items covered to keep track of when meetings occurred, who was present, high level discussion notes and follow-up actions.

tasks.title: Validate Network or IoT Incident
tasks.content: Network incidents refer to security events that occur at the network layer including unauthorized network access, network scanning, sniffing or eavesdropping. IoT incidents involve compromised IoT devices such as smart cameras, sensors, or other connected devices.
outcomes: A security incident is confirmed as a network or IoT incident

subtasks.title: Review network logs
subtasks.content: Reviewed network logs, firewall logs, IDS/IPS alerts, and router logs for suspicious activity.

subtasks.title: Analyze IoT device behavior
subtasks.content: Analyzed IoT device behavior for anomalies including unexpected network connections, unusual data transfers, or changes in device configuration.

subtasks.title: Check for unauthorized access
subtasks.content: Checked for signs of unauthorized network access including unknown MAC addresses, rogue access points, or unauthorized VPN connections.

subtasks.title: Verify network scanning activity
subtasks.content: Verified whether network scanning or reconnaissance activity has been detected.

tasks.title: Determine Scope of Incident
tasks.content: Identify all affected network segments, devices, and systems. It is important to collect as much relevant information as possible about all assets that are affected.
outcomes: All affected network segments are identified
All affected devices and systems are identified

subtasks.title: Identify affected network segments
subtasks.content: Identified which network segments were affected by reviewing network topology maps, VLAN configurations, and firewall rules.

subtasks.title: Catalog affected devices
subtasks.content: Cataloged all affected devices including servers, workstations, IoT devices, network equipment, and their criticality to business operations.

subtasks.title: Analyze network traffic
subtasks.content: Analyzed network traffic patterns using packet capture tools and network monitoring systems to identify anomalous communications.

subtasks.title: Review device inventory
subtasks.content: Reviewed device inventory and compared against known authorized devices to identify rogue or unauthorized devices on the network.

tasks.title: Assess Need for External Support
tasks.content: After an initial assessment of the scope of the network or IoT incident, the organization will have to make a determination as to whether outside help is needed and for what functions.
outcomes: Decision is made whether to hire external technical help
Decision is made whether to hire external legal help
ORNA incident response expertise is utilized if needed

subtasks.title: Consider network forensics expert
subtasks.content: Considered retaining a network forensics expert to help analyze network traffic and identify attack vectors.

subtasks.title: Consider IoT security specialist
subtasks.content: Considered retaining an IoT security specialist if IoT devices are involved in the incident.

subtasks.title: Consider outside legal counsel
subtasks.content: Considered retaining outside legal counsel if sensitive data or regulatory compliance is involved.

subtasks.title: Consider ORNA support
subtasks.content: Considered retaining ORNA for incident response expertise.

tasks.title: Assess Impact
tasks.content: Assess the impact to prioritize actions. The impacts should be assessed for operational business impact, regulatory impact, and reputational impact.

subtasks.title: Assess business impact
subtasks.content: Assessed impact to business or mission: How critical are the affected network segments and devices? How much disruption has occurred? What services are degraded or unavailable?

subtasks.title: Assess data compromise
subtasks.content: Assessed whether any sensitive data was accessed, exfiltrated, or compromised through network or IoT devices.

subtasks.title: Assess regulatory impact
subtasks.content: Assessed regulatory impact: What is the regulatory status of any compromised data (e.g., PII, ePHI)?

subtasks.title: Assess reputational damage
subtasks.content: Assessed potential reputational damage from the network or IoT security incident.

tasks.title: Determine Root Cause(s)
tasks.content: Identify how the network or IoT incident occurred. Understanding the root cause is critical for effective remediation.

subtasks.title: Analyze attack vectors
subtasks.content: Analyzed potential attack vectors including: weak or default credentials on IoT devices, unpatched vulnerabilities in network devices, misconfigured firewalls or access controls, rogue access points, or compromised VPN credentials.

subtasks.title: Review authentication logs
subtasks.content: Reviewed authentication logs to determine if unauthorized access occurred and how credentials were obtained.

subtasks.title: Check for exploited vulnerabilities
subtasks.content: Checked whether known vulnerabilities in network devices or IoT firmware were exploited.

subtasks.title: Identify configuration weaknesses
subtasks.content: Identified configuration weaknesses including default passwords, unnecessary open ports, or missing network segmentation.

tasks.title: Send Communications
tasks.content: Communications will need to exist for both internal and external parties. Timely and controlled external communications play a crucial role in managing the narrative.
outcomes: Approved external parties are contacted and notified of incident
Decision made whether to utilize ORNA templates for communication
Employees are notified about the incident and instructed on the next steps

subtasks.title: Work with Legal Counsel
subtasks.content: Worked with Legal Counsel and provided all available details about the incident severity, impact and the type of information involved, if known.

subtasks.title: Craft external messages
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel and Communications Team to craft messages to external parties to be notified.

subtasks.title: Craft internal messages
subtasks.content: Worked closely with Legal Counsel and Communications Team to craft messages for internal staff.

subtasks.title: Utilize ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Utilized ORNA communications templates where appropriate.

subtasks.title: Publish external communications
subtasks.content: Published external communications in line with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Work on communications cadence
subtasks.content: Worked with communications team and legal counsel on a cadence for updating external and internal communications.

tasks.title: Invoke Data Breach Notification Protocols
tasks.content: When a cyber incident is suspected or confirmed that may involve data compromise, it is important that the Legal Counsel proceeds swiftly to understand the type of data affected.
outcomes: The type of breach and affected data are established and confirmed
Appropriate data breach notification protocols are invoked

subtasks.title: Confirm data impact
subtasks.content: Confirmed whether Personal Identifiable Information (PII), Protected Health Information (PHI), or financial information is impacted.

subtasks.title: Examine regulatory obligations
subtasks.content: Examined regulatory obligations.

subtasks.title: Notify appropriate parties
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals as prescribed by relevant laws, regulations and contractual obligations.

subtasks.title: Advise on client-attorney privilege
subtasks.content: Advised the CIRT members on proper communication methods to ensure continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

phases.name: Containment
phases.description: The main objective of the Containment stage is to limit the impact of the incident promptly and as much as possible, preventing further damage to the organization's assets.

tasks.title: Isolate Affected Network Segments and Devices
tasks.content: Immediately isolate affected network segments and devices to prevent lateral movement and further compromise.

subtasks.title: Isolate network segments
subtasks.content: Isolated affected network segments using VLANs, firewall rules, or physical disconnection to prevent lateral movement.

subtasks.title: Disconnect compromised devices
subtasks.content: Disconnected compromised IoT devices and systems from the network while preserving forensic evidence.

subtasks.title: Block malicious traffic
subtasks.content: Configured firewalls and IPS to block known malicious IP addresses and traffic patterns.

subtasks.title: Preserve evidence
subtasks.content: Preserved forensic evidence before making changes to network configurations.

tasks.title: Disable Compromised Accounts
tasks.content: Disable any user or service accounts that have been compromised or are suspected of being compromised.

subtasks.title: Identify compromised accounts
subtasks.content: Identified compromised user and service accounts through log analysis.

subtasks.title: Disable accounts
subtasks.content: Disabled compromised accounts in Active Directory, LDAP, or other authentication systems.

subtasks.title: Revoke access tokens
subtasks.content: Revoked any active sessions, tokens, or certificates associated with compromised accounts.

tasks.title: Block Malicious Network Traffic
tasks.content: Block malicious network traffic at the perimeter and internal network boundaries.
outcomes: Command and control communications are disrupted

subtasks.title: Update firewall rules
subtasks.content: Updated firewall rules to block known malicious IP addresses, domains, and ports.

subtasks.title: Configure IPS signatures
subtasks.content: Configured IPS/IDS signatures to detect and block attack traffic.

subtasks.title: Block C2 communications
subtasks.content: Blocked command and control (C2) server communications if malware or botnet activity is detected.

subtasks.title: Update DNS filtering
subtasks.content: Updated DNS filtering to block known malicious domains.

tasks.title: Invoke Business Continuity Plan
tasks.content: To avoid major disruptions of key business functions, invoke the Business Continuity Plan (BCP), if such exists, in accordance with the incident severity and impact.
outcomes: If it exists the Business Continuity Plan is invoked
If no Business Continuity Plan exists a war room is used to create one
Situational business continuity procedures are established

subtasks.title: Invoke BCP communication
subtasks.content: Issued the communication and informed the appropriate stakeholders that the BCP should be invoked to minimize business disruption.

subtasks.title: Proceed with BCP procedures
subtasks.content: Proceeded in accordance with the BCP procedures.

subtasks.title: Establish war room if needed
subtasks.content: Established a war room if no BCP existed for affected systems and networks.

tasks.title: Issue External and Internal Communications Update
tasks.content: Issuing periodic updates on the progress of the incident response activities will help control the narrative.
outcomes: Internal stakeholders are informed about the ongoing containment measures
External stakeholders are informed about the ongoing containment measures

subtasks.title: Issue internal communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to internal stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Issue external communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to external stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

tasks.title: Issue Mandatory Regulatory Notifications or Updates
tasks.content: Issuing periodic updates about the progress of the incident response activities may be required based on the incident severity and the applicable laws, regulations and contractual obligations.
outcomes: Applicable internal and external parties are informed about the ongoing containment measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

phases.name: Eradication
phases.description: The Eradication stage focuses on removing all traces of the threat from the affected assets, and ensuring that the root cause has been eliminated.

tasks.title: Remove Unauthorized Devices and Access Points
tasks.content: Remove any unauthorized or rogue devices, access points, or network equipment from the network.
outcomes: All unauthorized devices are removed from the network

subtasks.title: Scan for unauthorized devices
subtasks.content: Scanned the network for unauthorized devices using network discovery tools and MAC address filtering.

subtasks.title: Remove rogue devices
subtasks.content: Physically removed or disabled rogue access points, unauthorized switches, or other network equipment.

subtasks.title: Update device inventory
subtasks.content: Updated the authorized device inventory and implemented controls to prevent unauthorized device connections.

tasks.title: Patch and Update Vulnerable Systems
tasks.content: Apply security patches and updates to all vulnerable network devices, IoT devices, and systems.

subtasks.title: Identify vulnerable systems
subtasks.content: Identified all vulnerable network devices, routers, switches, firewalls, and IoT devices requiring patches.

subtasks.title: Apply security patches
subtasks.content: Applied security patches to network infrastructure and IoT devices according to vendor recommendations.

subtasks.title: Update IoT firmware
subtasks.content: Updated firmware on all IoT devices to the latest secure versions.

subtasks.title: Verify patch deployment
subtasks.content: Verified that all patches were successfully deployed and systems are functioning properly.

tasks.title: Reset Compromised Credentials
tasks.content: Reset all credentials that were compromised or potentially exposed during the incident.

subtasks.title: Reset user passwords
subtasks.content: Reset passwords for all compromised user accounts.

subtasks.title: Reset IoT device credentials
subtasks.content: Changed default credentials on all IoT devices to strong, unique passwords.

subtasks.title: Reset service account credentials
subtasks.content: Reset service account credentials and API keys that may have been exposed.

subtasks.title: Implement MFA
subtasks.content: Implemented multi-factor authentication (MFA) for network access where not already in place.

tasks.title: Harden Network and IoT Configurations
tasks.content: Implement security hardening measures on network devices and IoT systems to prevent future incidents.

subtasks.title: Implement network segmentation
subtasks.content: Implemented or improved network segmentation to isolate IoT devices and critical systems.

subtasks.title: Disable unnecessary services
subtasks.content: Disabled unnecessary services, ports, and protocols on network devices and IoT systems.

subtasks.title: Configure access controls
subtasks.content: Configured strict access controls and implemented the principle of least privilege.

subtasks.title: Enable security logging
subtasks.content: Enabled comprehensive security logging on all network devices and IoT systems.

tasks.title: Remove Malware and Backdoors
tasks.content: If malware or backdoors were installed during the incident, ensure they are completely removed from all affected systems.

subtasks.title: Scan for malware
subtasks.content: Performed comprehensive malware scans on all affected network devices and systems.

subtasks.title: Remove identified threats
subtasks.content: Removed or quarantined all identified malware, rootkits, and backdoors.

subtasks.title: Factory reset IoT devices
subtasks.content: Performed factory reset on compromised IoT devices if malware cannot be reliably removed.

subtasks.title: Verify clean state
subtasks.content: Verified that systems are clean through multiple scanning methods and behavioral analysis.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: The Recovery stage aims to restore the affected assets to their full business-as-usual operation in the shortest possible time frame, after eradicating all traces of the threat.

tasks.title: Restore Network Services
tasks.content: Gradually restore network services and connectivity while monitoring for any signs of recurring issues.

subtasks.title: Test network connectivity
subtasks.content: Tested network connectivity and services in isolated segments before full restoration.

subtasks.title: Gradually restore services
subtasks.content: Gradually restored network services starting with the most critical business functions.

subtasks.title: Monitor for issues
subtasks.content: Continuously monitored restored services for any signs of recurring issues or anomalies.

subtasks.title: Verify functionality
subtasks.content: Verified that all network services are functioning as expected and performance is normal.

tasks.title: Reconnect IoT Devices
tasks.content: Safely reconnect IoT devices to the network after ensuring they are properly secured and configured.

subtasks.title: Verify IoT device security
subtasks.content: Verified that all IoT devices are properly patched, configured, and have strong credentials before reconnection.

subtasks.title: Reconnect to isolated network
subtasks.content: Reconnected IoT devices to a properly segmented and monitored network segment.

subtasks.title: Test device functionality
subtasks.content: Tested each IoT device to ensure it functions properly and does not exhibit suspicious behavior.

subtasks.title: Monitor device activity
subtasks.content: Implemented continuous monitoring of IoT device network activity and behavior.

tasks.title: Re-enable User Access
tasks.content: Re-enable user access to network resources after verifying that the environment is secure.

subtasks.title: Re-enable user accounts
subtasks.content: Re-enabled previously disabled user accounts after password resets and security verification.

subtasks.title: Verify MFA enrollment
subtasks.content: Verified that all users have enrolled in multi-factor authentication if newly implemented.

subtasks.title: Grant phased access
subtasks.content: Granted access in phases, starting with critical users and monitoring for any issues.

subtasks.title: Communicate access procedures
subtasks.content: Communicated new access procedures and security requirements to all users.

tasks.title: Validate Security Controls
tasks.content: Validate that all security controls are functioning properly and providing adequate protection.

subtasks.title: Test firewall rules
subtasks.content: Tested firewall rules to ensure they are properly blocking unauthorized traffic.

subtasks.title: Verify IDS/IPS functionality
subtasks.content: Verified that IDS/IPS systems are detecting and blocking attack patterns.

subtasks.title: Test access controls
subtasks.content: Tested network access controls and segmentation to ensure proper isolation.

subtasks.title: Validate logging and monitoring
subtasks.content: Validated that logging and monitoring systems are capturing all necessary security events.

tasks.title: Issue External and Internal Communications Updates
tasks.content: Once the affected systems are restored and the organization returns to Business As Usual (BAU), issue communications updates.
outcomes: External and internal communication updates are issued

subtasks.title: Issue internal communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to internal stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

subtasks.title: Issue external communications
subtasks.content: Issued the communications update to external stakeholders in accordance with the communications strategy.

tasks.title: Issue Mandatory Regulatory Notifications or Updates
tasks.content: Issuing regular updates about the progress of the incident resolution activities may be required based on the incident severity and the applicable laws and regulations.
outcomes: Applicable parties are informed about the ongoing recovery measures

subtasks.title: Notify regulatory agencies
subtasks.content: Notified the appropriate regulatory agencies and affected individuals in accordance with the applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Advise on privilege
subtasks.content: Continued advising the CIRT members on proper communications methods to ensure maintaining continuous client-attorney privilege where possible.

subtasks.title: Use ORNA templates
subtasks.content: Referred to the ORNA Communications Templates section for guidance on the wording.

tasks.title: Resolve Incident
tasks.content: Once the affected systems and network are restored to normal business operations, the incident can be moved to the 'resolved' state.
outcomes: The incident is declared as resolved

subtasks.title: Verify systems back to normal
subtasks.content: Verified with Core CIRT Members and IT staff that all systems and network services are back to normal business functions.

subtasks.title: Change incident status
subtasks.content: Changed status of incident to 'resolved'.

phases.name: Lessons Learned
phases.description: The Lessons Learned stage's main goal is to compile all relevant information about the incident and extract lessons that can help with future incident response activity.

tasks.title: Generate Incident Report
tasks.content: Preparing an incident report is an important part of the cyber incident response process. This report will be used to perform business impact analysis and comply with applicable laws and regulations.

subtasks.title: Generate report
subtasks.content: Once the Incident is marked as Resolved, used the Reports tab to generate and view the report.

subtasks.title: Review for accuracy
subtasks.content: Reviewed the report for accuracy and addressed any errors/issues with the team member(s) responsible for the task in question.

subtasks.title: Ensure artifacts included
subtasks.content: Ensured all relevant artifacts are included for each task.

subtasks.title: Share report
subtasks.content: Downloaded or exported the report and shared it with the appropriate stakeholders.

subtasks.title: Retain report
subtasks.content: Ensured that the Incident report and all related evidence are retained in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements.

tasks.title: Fulfill External and Internal Reporting Obligations
tasks.content: Once the incident is resolved and the investigation is complete, deliver the incident report to required parties.
outcomes: The Incident Report is shared with applicable parties
Regulatory and legal obligations are fulfilled

subtasks.title: Obtain and review report
subtasks.content: Obtained the full Incident Report from the CIRT Leader and reviewed it for accuracy.

subtasks.title: Identify obligations
subtasks.content: Identified all applicable legal and regulatory requirements related to cyber incident reporting.

subtasks.title: Deliver report
subtasks.content: Delivered the Incident report to the appropriate legal and/or regulatory agencies and affected individuals.

tasks.title: Host the Lessons Learned Session
tasks.content: The goal of the Lessons Learned session is to learn from the incident and identify policy, process or procedure gaps.
outcomes: The Lessons Learned session is conducted
Gaps and areas for improvement are identified
Initiatives are created to operationalize identified improvements

subtasks.title: Prepare presentation
subtasks.content: Prepared a presentation covering: when incident was detected, scope, response steps taken, areas where CIRT was effective, gaps and improvement opportunities, prevention steps.

subtasks.title: Host session
subtasks.content: Hosted the Lessons Learned session with all CIRT members actively participating.

subtasks.title: Establish open dialogue
subtasks.content: Established an open dialogue discussing: how well staff handled incident, if procedures were followed and adequate, what held back recovery, what needs to be done differently.

subtasks.title: Document improvements
subtasks.content: Documented all identified areas for improvement and prepared a list of agreed upon action items.

subtasks.title: Distribute action items
subtasks.content: Distributed the action items list among relevant CIRT members and ensured that the improvement steps are implemented.

tasks.title: Perform Employee Security Awareness Training
tasks.content: Conduct employee awareness training after the incident is resolved to prevent similar future incidents.
outcomes: A roadmap for table top exercises is developed

subtasks.title: Prepare educational materials
subtasks.content: Prepared educational materials emphasizing network and IoT security best practices.

subtasks.title: Track attendance
subtasks.content: Tracked employee attendance using a Learning Management System (LMS).

subtasks.title: Work with 3rd party
subtasks.content: Where applicable, worked with a 3rd party to prepare and conduct the employee security awareness training.

subtasks.title: Develop table top exercises
subtasks.content: Used the outcomes from the Lessons Learned to develop table top exercise plans with relevant scenarios.
